mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to downward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of months where UNRATE > 7.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series rise concurrently, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects improving factory utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term risk-on positioning and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230.0, markets anticipate aggressive monetary tightening or loss of central bank credibility, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 230.0 coincides with positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, or the interaction term is insignificant in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate profitability, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals supply bottlenecks and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward testing.
